title: City of Syracuse seeks to close nuisance convenience store in Outer Comstock neighborhood
summary: The City of Syracuse is pursuing revocation of a special use permit for Market convenience store in Outer Comstock neighborhood, citing illegal cannabis and vape sales, tobacco license violations, and quality-of-life complaints from residents. A public hearing is scheduled for August 27, 2025. The case demonstrates code enforcement action and community concern about nuisance retail operations near schools and parks.

political_signal.description: Community complaints about illegal retail operations, parking violations, and litter driving formal city enforcement action with public hearing process.

processing_notes: Deputy Commissioner title suggests Jake Dishaw works in Code Enforcement Department
Article references multiple enforcement agencies: state (cannabis), county (health/vapes), city (code enforcement, tobacco)
Proximity to Syracuse Latin School and Syracuse University suggests intergovernmental coordination may be relevant
Special use permit revocation is formal legal process; public hearing date (8/27) is actionable milestone
